pending.body: finding_summary: `milestone next --help` and `milestone run --help` now say they operate on the next "unblocked" bead, but the implementation intentionally treats retryable blocked beads as actionable too. The new long_about text at `src/cli/milestone.rs:84` and `src/cli/milestone.rs:93` therefore misdocuments the real controller behavior: `handle_next` surfaces blocked-retry beads via `inspect_next_milestone_action` (`src/cli/milestone.rs:667`, `src/cli/milestone.rs:897-975`), and `execute_milestone_run` will resume them (`src/cli/milestone.rs:1014-1077`). The regression test at `src/cli/milestone.rs:3382-3464` already codifies that blocked retry beads are still actionable. remediation_hint: change these help strings to "next actionable bead" or explicitly mention retryable failed beads instead of saying "unblocked".

finding_summary: the new `task` help text is internally inconsistent about legacy standalone-project compatibility. `task --help` explicitly says standalone projects remain supported (`src/cli/task.rs:16-18`), but the new per-subcommand descriptions for `show`, `select`, and `list` are task-only (`src/cli/task.rs:32-52`) even though those commands still show/select/list standalone projects. The updated compatibility tests at `tests/cli.rs:19443-19465` and `tests/cli.rs:19635-19702` confirm that standalone projects remain part of the supported behavior. As shipped, operators reading subcommand help will be told these commands are task-only when they are not. remediation_hint: either mention standalone-project compatibility in those subcommand descriptions or avoid wording like "active task" / "List known tasks" that implies non-task projects are unsupported.

status_summary: running: final review restart round 7 -> Plan and Implement
